plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure for capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Hold 15% cash buffer for stability.
Watch TSLA for a potential small 5% buy if it triggers.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for approximately 1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and limiting concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged for exit.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to lower leveraged ETF exposure.
Add to CRWD, a strong AI/Software name, to 15%.
Buy TSLA for 5% of the book, a high-quality growth setup.
Increase QQQI to 22% for balanced income and broad exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly return, focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, aiming for a moderate risk profile with controlled drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged for exit.
Increase SPAL to 5% for leveraged exposure, staying within limits.
Add to CRWD, concentrating in this strong AI/Software name to 20%.
Buy TSLA for 10% of the book, a high-scoring growth setup.
Buy NVDU for 5%, another strong scanner setup for tech exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for approximately 4% monthly return, pursuing stretched growth. It involves higher leverage and concentration, increasing both potential upside and significant drawdown risk. Leveraged ETFs like SPAL are subject to decay and rebalancing, amplifying daily moves.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income-generating exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift towards income focus.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Hold a 15% cash buffer for stability and potential distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% monthly return, prioritizing steady distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs, with minimal exposure to high-beta single names and no leveraged ETFs. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
